Genesis.author: Traditionally Moses
Genesis.impact: Introduces major themes: creation, covenant, and origins of Israel; widely cited in theology and literature.

Exodus.author: Traditionally Moses
Exodus.impact: Central to Jewish identity and law; influential for concepts of liberation and law in Western thought.

Psalms.author: Various (David, Asaph, others)
Psalms.celebrity: Many beloved prayers and hymns (e.g., Psalm 23)
Psalms.impact: Core of Jewish and Christian worship; strong presence in liturgy, music, and devotional life.

Isaiah.author: Isaiah (traditionally)
Isaiah.celebrity: Messianic prophecies (often cited in Christian readings)
Isaiah.impact: Major prophetic voice shaping Messianic expectations and ethical critique of rulers.

Matthew.author: Traditionally Matthew the apostle
Matthew.impact: A primary Gospel shaping Christian ethics and Christology; widely used in teaching and liturgy.

John.author: Traditionally John the apostle
John.impact: Deep theological portrait of Jesus; influential in doctrinal formulations and devotional reading.

Romans.author: Paul the Apostle
Romans.celebrity: Systematic exposition of justification by faith
Romans.impact: Foundational to Christian theology, especially doctrines of salvation and grace; pivotal in Reformation history.

Revelation.author: John (traditionally)
Revelation.impact: Provokes wide-ranging interpretations about prophecy and eschatology; rich in symbolic art and literature.
